title: trim_end_matches('"') strips multiple trailing quotes in Signature header parser
description: In signatures.rs:115 and 117, `parse_signature_header()` extracts quoted values with `.trim_end_matches('"')`. `trim_end_matches` removes ALL matching characters from the end, not just one. A Signature header value like `headers="(request-target) host""" ` (with extra trailing quotes inside the token) would have those quotes stripped, silently accepting a malformed header. More critically, a value like `signature="abc"xyz"` would produce `abc"xyz` after stripping only the final quote — but `trim_end_matches` would leave `abc"xyz` which includes an unintended embedded quote, potentially misidentifying the signature bytes. The correct fix is to use a proper quoted-string parser: find the closing `"` by scanning forward from after the opening quote (respecting the split already done), not by stripping from the end. At minimum, replace with `.strip_suffix('"').unwrap_or(val)` to remove exactly one trailing quote.
close_reason: P1-P3 fixes: unwrap replaced, INSERT OR IGNORE dedup, X-Real-IP, zeroizing noted, prune wired, strip_suffix, SSRF guard, shared client, dead code removed, issue refs added
